raw_output:
```
══════════════════════════════════════════════
  CODE REVIEW REPORT
  Files changed: 1 | Lines added: +78 | Lines removed: -0
══════════════════════════════════════════════

── BLOCKERS (2) ────────────────────────────

[B1] src/api/posts.ts:24 — Off-by-one error in pagination offset calculation
     The offset is calculated as `page * limit`, but pages are 1-indexed (as documented
     in the comment). This means page=1 would skip the first 20 posts (offset=20 instead
     of offset=0). Page=2 would skip 40 posts instead of 20. This breaks pagination entirely.
     Fix: Change line 24 to `const offset = (page - 1) * limit;`
     Same issue exists on line 74 in getPostsByTag.

[B2] src/api/posts.ts:14-17 — Missing validation for negative or zero page/limit values
     If a user sends `page=0`, `page=-1`, or `limit=-5`, the code will calculate a negative
     or zero offset/limit and pass it to the database, causing either incorrect results or
     a database error. The `|| 1` fallback only handles NaN, not negative numbers.
     Fix: Add validation after parsing:
     ```
     const page = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.page as string) || 1);
     const limit = Math.max(1, Math.min(parseInt(req.query.limit as string) || DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, MAX_PAGE_SIZE));
     ```

── WARNINGS (3) ────────────────────────────

[W1] src/api/posts.ts:49,64 — No input sanitization for slug/tag parameters
     The `slug` and `tag` values from `req.params` are passed directly to database queries
     without validation. If the database driver doesn't properly escape these values, this
     could enable NoSQL injection attacks (e.g., passing `{"$ne": null}` as a slug).
     Suggestion: Validate that slug/tag are non-empty strings matching expected patterns, or
     ensure your database driver is configured to prevent injection attacks.

[W2] src/api/posts.ts:14,48,64 — No error handling for database query failures
     All three functions call `db.posts` methods without try/catch blocks. If the database
     connection fails or a query error occurs, the unhandled promise rejection will crash
     the process or return a generic 500 error with no logging.
     Suggestion: Wrap database calls in try/catch and return appropriate error responses.

[W3] src/api/posts.ts:1-78 — No test coverage for new API module
     This is a new file with no accompanying test file. Pagination logic, edge cases
     (invalid params, missing posts), and error paths should all have test coverage.
     Suggestion: Add tests for happy paths, 404 responses, and invalid pagination parameters.

── SUGGESTIONS (2) ─────────────────────────

[S1] src/api/posts.ts:20,72 — totalCount query on every request may be expensive
     Counting all published posts on every paginated request could become a performance
     bottleneck on large datasets. Consider caching this value with a short TTL or using
     approximate counts for non-critical pagination metadata.

[S2] src/api/posts.ts:82-83 — Inconsistent response formatting
     The pagination object is formatted on a single line here, while the same object in
     getPosts (lines 32-40) is formatted across multiple lines. Maintain consistency.

── VERDICT ─────────────────────────────────

  ✗ NOT READY — 2 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging.

══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

```
── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ────────────────────────
{"blockers": 2, "warnings": 3, "suggestions": 2, "verdict": "NOT_READY"}
────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

blockers.title: src/api/posts.ts:24 — Off-by-one error in pagination offset calculation
blockers.body:
The offset is calculated as `page * limit`, but pages are 1-indexed (as documented
in the comment). This means page=1 would skip the first 20 posts (offset=20 instead
of offset=0). Page=2 would skip 40 posts instead of 20. This breaks pagination entirely.
Fix: Change line 24 to `const offset = (page - 1) * limit;`
Same issue exists on line 74 in getPostsByTag.
blockers.raw:
src/api/posts.ts:24 — Off-by-one error in pagination offset calculation
     The offset is calculated as `page * limit`, but pages are 1-indexed (as documented
     in the comment). This means page=1 would skip the first 20 posts (offset=20 instead
     of offset=0). Page=2 would skip 40 posts instead of 20. This breaks pagination entirely.
     Fix: Change line 24 to `const offset = (page - 1) * limit;`
     Same issue exists on line 74 in getPostsByTag.

blockers.title: src/api/posts.ts:14-17 — Missing validation for negative or zero page/limit values
blockers.body:
If a user sends `page=0`, `page=-1`, or `limit=-5`, the code will calculate a negative
or zero offset/limit and pass it to the database, causing either incorrect results or
a database error. The `|| 1` fallback only handles NaN, not negative numbers.
Fix: Add validation after parsing:
```
const page = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.page as string) || 1);
const limit = Math.max(1, Math.min(parseInt(req.query.limit as string) || DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, MAX_PAGE_SIZE));
```

     If a user sends `page=0`, `page=-1`, or `limit=-5`, the code will calculate a negative

warnings.title: src/api/posts.ts:49,64 — No input sanitization for slug/tag parameters
warnings.body:
The `slug` and `tag` values from `req.params` are passed directly to database queries
without validation. If the database driver doesn't properly escape these values, this
could enable NoSQL injection attacks (e.g., passing `{"$ne": null}` as a slug).
Suggestion: Validate that slug/tag are non-empty strings matching expected patterns, or
ensure your database driver is configured to prevent injection attacks.

     ensure your database driver is configured to prevent injection attacks.

warnings.title: src/api/posts.ts:14,48,64 — No error handling for database query failures
warnings.body:
All three functions call `db.posts` methods without try/catch blocks. If the database
connection fails or a query error occurs, the unhandled promise rejection will crash
the process or return a generic 500 error with no logging.
Suggestion: Wrap database calls in try/catch and return appropriate error responses.

suggestions.title: src/api/posts.ts:20,72 — totalCount query on every request may be expensive
suggestions.body:
Counting all published posts on every paginated request could become a performance
bottleneck on large datasets. Consider caching this value with a short TTL or using
approximate counts for non-critical pagination metadata.
